hall_of_fame.induction_speech:
Our first inductee into our Hall of Flame, with great pride and honor, we would like to present the best Linebacker this organization will ever see, jonnyboy1207.

No better canidate could we have asked for to step foot in our organization. Working with him daily was the highlight of my career and I wish Jonnyboy the best when it comes to future endeavors.
Thank you, Jonnyboy.

Our Second inductee into our Hall of Flame, Hammy.

Our founding father and the best Bench Warmer this organization will ever see. Not a single other person enters my mind when I think of who I wanted to build a Slapshot Team with. Hammy might not have been with the franchise too long but his impression will live on forever through us.

Thank you, Hammy.

Our Third inductee into our Hall of Flame, MDub.

Our most promising prospect this franchise had to date entered into retirement early due to the consumption of shampoo. We wish him well with his future endevours and future oportunities in his young slapshot career. May one day he make pro and all of us proud.

Thank you, Mdub.

Our Fourth Inductee into the Hall of Flame, Dizruption.

It is our honor to showcase one of our most prominent playmakers to date. Whether it be psychological warfare or helping usher the franchise into the era it stands now. Without him the Tamales would not have found the home they did in pro of Season 8. Showing up early and leaving late to nearly every practice in your time in Hot Springs, we will never forget you.

We thank you Dizruption for your work ethic, your talent, and the impression you have brought to and left on our facilities. May fortune be as kind to you as you were to us.

Our Fifth Inductee into the Hall of Flame, Zartholomew.

The physical embodiment of 'He Shoots, He Scores.' Zartholomew has been a dominating force on the Hot Springs offense. At the time of this announcement, he has scored the most goals out of everyone who dawned the Flames. He may have only gotten to spend 3 seasons with us, but his legacy will be felt by everyone around the organization for years to come. I'll never forget the day we were walking down Seneca Drive when I tripped and fell onto the train tracks beside the road as a train was coming, only to have Zartholomew scoop me up and carry me to safety as the train came barreling by. We thank you for your on-ice and off-ice efforts that have blessed this organization day in and day out.

We appreciate everything you brought to our table—the competitive atmosphere, the hunger to win, the edge to drive on. We hope that your next chapter in life, whether it's on SPL ice or not, will show you the same kindness you showed us.

memberships.notes: First adopted team by Hot Springs that didnt use the Hot Springs Location

hall_of_fame.induction_speech: Ottawa Valley is proud to announce the very first jersey to be retired in franchise history. Patalie Nortman was a cornerstone in the Puck Slappers' transition from being a middling open team to the successful team they are now. Patalie Nortman always brought the energy in the locker room and knew how to get the team hyped up whenever they needed it! Although his time on the team was short, every single moment in his career was memorable. From his big three goal debut to his HUGE playoff goals, Patalie Nortman was one of the most exciting players in Ottawa Valley history.

Ottawa Valley is proud to announce that YmisScarab's jersey will be joining Patalie Nortman and Tee's in the rafter. YmisScarab's Ottawa Valley career started in Season 13 and would last until Season 17 with stops on the Stick Slappers, Post Dingers, Wood Slappers and the Beauties. Scarab started off his Ottawa Valley career hot with a hattrick in 74 seconds, he also owns the Ottawa Valley record for most saves in a single season. Scarab has been a huge part of the behind the scenes working on Ottawa Valley statistics, tournaments and upkeeping the 6v6 Leagues. Although he has hung up his skates, Scarab remains committed to keeping our tournaments going and making sure all of our spreadsheets properly function. Scarab has been the silent backbone of the franchise for a long time and his help has always been appreciated.

It is with the greatest honor that I, alongside the Ottaway Valley, will be announcing another jersey immortalized in the rafters, @TEE . An icon since the birth of Slapshot, on the ice, and off it too. He's been the center-piece of every play and shows his strong leadership while also being a lovable teammate and even more endearing friend. Even though he believes he 'passed his prime' he still put up elite numbers till the last game he played. As of recent with the Ottawa Valley Puck Slappers he won multiple awards for the team, but also as an individual player. I've watched Tee as an idol of mine early on, then later being trained under his wing to reach my fullest potential, but the whole time Tee and I became closer friends then ever bringing a friendship beyond the games we play. The days we spent together with other members of the Ottawa Valley especially in the 6v6 League will be memories I keep with me forever. From the elite banner he carries on his back to his infinitely rewatchable youtube videos, Tee's name will live forever amongst the community regardless of where the wind takes him.

hall_of_fame.induction_speech: The first number to ever be retired in the typhoons history is #43. The #43 was the number of the player with the most points on the typhoons for 2 seasons, Dizruption. Dizruption was also a big part of how the typhoons got to challenger.
The second number to be retired is #13. Skiztastic is the best scorer in typhoons history putting up 91 goals in 1 season and 113 total.

The third number to be retired is #92. N, joined the typhoons last second right before their first season in pro. N played in challenger and IM all his career. People were skeptical if N would be able to compete in pro. He showed what he was able to do by putting up 71 points with 24 goals and 47 assists. N hanged up the skates with the typhoons and will not be seen soon.
The leader is so many stats I couldn't list them all here, Ghostwalker more like Goatwalker, a goalscoring machine who was played at every level of Vader.
The first MVP in franchise history, Azz is the veteran, a team leader, and an absolute menace in a board battle.
The definition of hard work pays off, Huzz worked his way from JSA B team to captaining two SPL teams in one season, winning a chip along the way!
The greatest 3 in Vader history, his slick moves, incredible saves, and competitive edge makes him a force on the ice.
Just some guy, he's okay at making stuff look good off the ice.
A champion with multiple chips, great mechanics and the head of the Scouting Department.
The most well rounded among us, with life, personality, and amazing vibes.
The greatest pub pick-up in Vader History, inventor of the Chytil Maneuver and defintion of passion.
A menace to play against on any server but East, 3s everywhere hate him, and pubs fear him.
The Big A himself, master scouter, IM Chip winner, Central B finalist, who takes after Maurice Richard himself with his tenacity.
Once considered the best prospect Pro had ever seen, now one of the best Pro has ever seen; our very own superstar.

We are so proud to announce the first ever number retirement in Cowtown history. @Matikor , after saving the Guppies undefeated season this Sunday will have his 79 never worn again by anyone else in Cowtown. We congratulate him and his family on this honor. Please, if you see Matikor in the future. Give thanks. It is well deserved

We are so proud to announce the second ever number retirement in Cowtown history. @Masked , after saving the Bongfish from a forfeit this Saturday will have his 6 never worn again by anyone else in Cowtown. We congratulate him and his family on this honor. Please, if you see Masked in the future. Give thanks. It is well deserved.
Happy to be retiring Dyno Mike's number after an incredible year with the Guppies.
Ghetto has done an incredible job as not only a player in Cowtown, but a GM here.
For all the great contributions he helped make to the team we will be retiring Ghoul's number
Noob Weapons, the cornerstone of Cowtown, of course gets the numbered retired.
Renard basically bringing the current Guppies together deserves all the love in the world and will have his number retired.
Skimz was an easy choice for many reasons. Mostly his Cowtown segment on his podcast every episode.

Unfortunately, we were gearing up for Litfox's number retirement but someone who was vaping in an unapproved area was standing too close, and the cover for the banner caught on fire. We were only able to scavenge one piece from the burns. Since we sank all the rest of our money into the jerseys we released today, we cannot buy a new banner for Litfox. So, since his number or name can't be seen on the banner, anyone can still use the #77 in Cowtown! Thank you! Feel free to buy a Cowtown jersey this week!

Congratulations to @rxiderzz on having his number retired to the rafters! After an incredible season captaining the return of the Guplings, Milker will be hanging up the laces and joining our Alumni! What a memorable career and we are so honored having his name and number forever remembered! Everyone give Milker a big cowtown MOO!

Our first entry into the Orcas hall of fame is @JoeShmoe, although he was a short term addition he brought the Orcas through qualifiers and established our name into the scene, for this we thank you. The number 3 will be retired for no future orca to wear, thank you for your contribution.

Our second entry into the Orcas hall of fame is @MUGZRD. Mugzrd was a tough decision to bring in over the OG Joeshmoe. Although his impact wasn't in the numbers he made a real impact behind the scene and was always a great guy to run up pugs with. We retire the number 8 into the HOF and it will be used by no future orcas. Thank you Mugz!
The glue guy every locker room needs. The leader every front office wants. Men fear him. Women fear him. Fish fear him. Everyone fears him.
'The original number 69.' -DarthTaksil
A western hero. An individual every man should aspire to be. We live merely lives. This man lived a legacy.
The only Pro Slapshot player to be credited with a goal while playing with a Trackpad
